# Four Thousand Weeks - Life Calendar (Full Documentation)

> A Memento Mori life calendar that visualizes a human lifespan as a grid of approximately 4,000 weeks.

Inspired by Oliver Burkeman's book "Four Thousand Weeks: Time Management for Mortals" and the Stoic practice of Memento Mori — "remember that you will die." The concept was also popularized by Tim Urban's "Your Life in Weeks" blog post on Wait But Why.

Live App: https://four-thousand-weeks.chayuto.com/
Source Code: https://github.com/chayuto/four-thousand-weeks

## Overview

This is a single-page web application that renders a human lifespan (80 years) as a visual grid of weeks. Each row represents one year, and each column one of the 52 weeks in that year, producing a grid of approximately 4,160 cells. The grid provides an at-a-glance view of how much time has passed and how much remains, encouraging mindful living.

All data is stored locally in the user's browser using localStorage. There is no backend server, no user accounts, and no analytics or tracking. The application is fully client-side and privacy-first.

## Core Features

### Life Grid Visualization
- 52 columns (weeks per year) × 80 rows (years of life) = ~4,160 interactive cells
- Three cell states: past (lived), current (this week, pulsing animation), future
- Hover any cell to see a tooltip with the date range and year/week number
- Click any cell to open an inline detail panel for adding events

### Life Events
- Point-in-time events: mark specific weeks with labels (e.g., "Graduated college", "Got married")
- Events are displayed as dots within their corresponding week cells
- Events include a title, date, optional color, and category

### Life Eras
- Time-range overlays: define spans of life with categories
- Six categories: Work, Education, Location, Relationship, Health, Other
- Each category has a distinct color for visual differentiation
- Eras can overlap (e.g., "College" and "Living in Boston" can cover the same period)
- Era colors are applied as background overlays on week cells

### Statistics (Header)
- Weeks lived (total count)
- Weeks remaining (based on 80-year life expectancy)
- Percentage of life completed

### Data Management
- Import/Export: Save and load life data as JSON files
- All persistence via browser localStorage
- Automatic Date object hydration on load

### Print Support
- Optimized print stylesheet for physical display
- Background colors preserved with print-color-adjust
- UI elements hidden in print mode
- Year rows respect page breaks

### Accessibility
- Keyboard navigation for week cells
- Focus-visible outlines
- ARIA labels on interactive elements
- Respects prefers-color-scheme for dark/light mode

## Technical Architecture

### Tech Stack
| Technology | Version | Purpose |
|------------|---------|---------|
| React | 19.x | UI library with concurrent features |
| TypeScript | 5.9.x | Strict type safety (noUncheckedIndexedAccess) |
| Vite | 7.x | Build tool with SWC (Rust-based compilation) |
| Tailwind CSS | 4.x | Utility-first CSS framework |
| Zustand | 5.x | Lightweight state management |
| date-fns | 4.x | Tree-shakeable date arithmetic |
| Zod | 3.x | Runtime data validation |

### Performance Optimizations
The app renders 4,000+ interactive DOM elements, requiring careful optimization:

1. **Native CSS Grid** with `contain: strict` for layout containment
2. **React.memo** on WeekCell prevents unnecessary re-renders
3. **Event Delegation** — single click/hover listener on the grid container, not on each cell
4. **Singleton Tooltip** — one tooltip instance that repositions, not 4,000 instances
5. **Pre-computed Week Map** — O(1) lookups for era/event associations per week

### Project Structure
```
src/
├── components/
│   ├── Header.tsx          — App header with stats and birthday editor
│   ├── BirthDateInput.tsx  — Initial birthday entry form
│   ├── LifeGrid.tsx        — Main grid with event delegation
│   ├── WeekCell.tsx        — Individual cell (React.memo optimized)
│   ├── WeekTooltip.tsx     — Singleton hover tooltip
│   ├── WeekDetailRow.tsx   — Inline detail panel (click-to-expand)
│   ├── EventPanel.tsx      — Bulk event management modal
│   ├── EventForm.tsx       — Event creation/editing form
│   └── EventList.tsx       — Event listing within panel
├── lib/
│   ├── dateUtils.ts        — date-fns wrapper for week/year calculations
│   ├── schemas.ts          — Zod schemas for data validation
│   └── importExport.ts     — JSON serialization/deserialization
├── store/
│   └── lifeCalendarStore.ts — Zustand store with localStorage persistence
├── types/
│   └── index.ts            — TypeScript type definitions
├── App.tsx                  — Root component
├── main.tsx                 — React entry point
└── index.css                — Tailwind imports + CSS design system
```

### Data Model

#### WeekData
Each cell in the grid is computed from:
- Year index (0–79) and week index (0–51)
- Start date and end date of the week
- Status: past, current, or future
- Associated events and eras (matched by date overlap)

#### LifeEvent
```typescript
{
  id: string;
  title: string;
  date: Date;
  color?: string;
  category?: 'work' | 'education' | 'location' | 'relationship' | 'health' | 'other';
}
```

#### LifeEra
```typescript
{
  id: string;
  title: string;
  startDate: Date;
  endDate: Date;
  color: string;
  category: 'work' | 'education' | 'location' | 'relationship' | 'health' | 'other';
}
```

### Design System
CSS custom properties for theming:

**Dark Mode (default):**
- Background: #0a0a0a
- Surface: #141414
- Past weeks: #3f3f46
- Current week: #fbbf24 (amber)
- Future weeks: #27272a

**Light Mode (prefers-color-scheme: light):**
- Background: #ffffff
- Surface: #f9fafb
- Past weeks: #6b7280
- Current week: #f59e0b
- Future weeks: #e5e7eb

**Era Category Colors:**
- Work: #3b82f6 (blue)
- Education: #8b5cf6 (purple)
- Location: #10b981 (green)
- Relationship: #f43f5e (rose)
- Health: #06b6d4 (cyan)
- Other: #6b7280 (gray)

## Deployment
- Hosted on GitHub Pages
- Custom domain: four-thousand-weeks.chayuto.com
- CI/CD via GitHub Actions (lint, typecheck, build, deploy)
- Build tool: Vite (output to dist/)

## Further Reading
- Oliver Burkeman: "Four Thousand Weeks: Time Management for Mortals"
- Tim Urban: "Your Life in Weeks" (Wait But Why)
- Stoic philosophy: Memento Mori
